    • 悉混然从口中涌出 - Means 'all came out in a mixed state from the mouth.'
    • 遂差 - Means 'cured' or 'recovered.'
    • 明期 - A personal name, likely a courtesy name.
    • 居豫章 - Means 'lived in Yuzhang' (an ancient name for Nanchang, Jiangxi).
    • 梅元龙 - A personal name, likely a courtesy name.
    • 太守 - Means 'governor' or 'prefect.'
    • 中恶腹满 - Means 'suddenly felt ill with a full abdomen.'
    • 索麝香丸 - Means 'asked for musk pills.'
    • 令作凶具 - Means 'ordered the preparation of funeral items.'
    • 二十七曰 - Means 'on the 27th day.'
    • 晡时 - Means 'late afternoon.'
    • 二十八曰 - Means 'on the 28th day.'
    • 卒 - Means 'servant' or 'attendant.'
    • 著丧衣 - Means 'wearing mourning clothes.'
    • 迎我 - Means 'to welcome me.'
    • 同梦如前 - Means 'had the same dream as before.'
    • 二十八曰当拜 - Means 'on the 28th day, I will be enfeoffed.'
    • 亡 - Means 'died.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 桓宣武 - Refers to Huan Wen, a prominent general and politician during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 督将 - Refers to military officers who were responsible for overseeing troops and managing logistics.
    • 因时行病后虚热 - A medical term describing a condition of lingering fever after an illness, which was common in ancient China.
    • 复茗 - Tea, which was a popular beverage in ancient China.
    • 斛二斗 - A large volume of liquid, equivalent to about 12 liters.
    • 升合 - Smaller units of liquid, where 1 sheng is about 0.2 liters and 1 he is about 0.02 liters.
    • 家贫 - Describes a poor family, which was a common theme in ancient Chinese literature.
    • 造之 - Visiting someone's home, which was a social custom in ancient China.
    • 闻世有此病 - Indicates that the condition was known but rare.
    • 令更进五升 - The act of forcing someone to drink more liquid, which was a common medical practice in ancient China.
    • 大吐 - Vomiting heavily, which was a common symptom of certain diseases.
    • 如升大 - Describing the size of the object, which is about the size of a sheng.
    • 有口 - Describing the object as having a mouth, which suggests it was a living creature.
    • 形质缩绉 - Describing the object as shrunken and wrinkled, which suggests it was a dried or shriveled form.
    • 状如牛肚 - Describing the object as looking like a cow's stomach, which suggests it was a large, hollow, and flexible object.
    • 悉混然从口中涌出 - Describing the object coming out in a mixed state from the mouth, which suggests it was a complex and unusual object.
    • 遂差 - Cured or recovered, which indicates a positive outcome.
    • 明期 - A personal name, likely a courtesy name, which was common in ancient China.
    • 居豫章 - Living in Yuzhang (Nanchang, Jiangxi), which was an important city in ancient China.
    • 梅元龙 - A personal name, likely a courtesy name, which was common in ancient China.
    • 太守 - Governor or prefect, which was a high-ranking official in ancient China.
    • 中恶腹满 - Sudden illness with a full abdomen, which was a common medical condition.
    • 索麝香丸 - Asking for musk pills, which were used for medicinal purposes.
    • 令作凶具 - Ordering the preparation of funeral items, which was a common practice in ancient China.
    • 二十七曰 - On the 27th day, which was a significant date in the lunar calendar.
    • 晡时 - Late afternoon, which was a specific time of day in ancient China.
    • 二十八曰 - On the 28th day, which was another significant date in the lunar calendar.
    • 卒 - Servant or attendant, which was a common social role in ancient China.
    • 著丧衣 - Wearing mourning clothes, which was a common practice in ancient China.
    • 迎我 - To welcome someone, which was a common social custom in ancient China.
    • 同梦如前 - Having the same dream as before, which was a common theme in ancient Chinese literature.
    • 二十八曰当拜 - On the 28th day, I will be enfeoffed, which was a significant event in ancient China.
    • 亡 - Died, which was a common theme in ancient Chinese literature.

    During the reign of Huan Wen, there was a military officer who, after suffering from a lingering fever, could drink even more tea. He would have to consume one hu and two dou to feel full. If the amount was reduced even slightly, he would feel it was insufficient. This was not a one-time occurrence. His family was poor. Later, a guest visited him and found him drinking tea. The guest had heard of this condition before and ordered him to drink five sheng more. The officer then vomited heavily, expelling an object about the size of a sheng, with a mouth, a shrunken and wrinkled form, and a shape resembling a cow's stomach. The guest placed the object in a basin and poured one hu and two dou of tea over it. The object absorbed all the tea and only felt slightly bloated. When another five sheng of tea was added, the object came out in a mixed state from his mouth. After expelling the object, the officer's illness was cured. Someone asked him, "What was this illness?" He replied, "It is called 'Hu'er Jia.'"

    Huan Zhe, courtesy name Mingqi, lived in Yuzhang when Mei Yuanlong was the governor. Mei had already fallen ill, and Huan went to visit him. He told Mei, "Last night, I suddenly dreamed of being a servant, welcoming you to become the Lord of Mount Tai." Mei was startled and said, "I also dreamed that you were a servant, wearing mourning clothes, coming to welcome me." After several days, they had the same dream again, and it was said that on the 28th day, he would be enfeoffed. On the 27th day at dusk, Huan suddenly fell ill with a full abdomen and asked Mei for musk pills. Upon hearing this, Mei ordered the preparation of funeral items. On the 27th day, Huan died; on the 28th day, Mei also passed away. [16]

    平原华歆，字子鱼，为诸生时，常宿人门外。主人妇夜产。有顷，两吏来诣其门，便相向辟易，欲退，却相谓曰：“公在此。”因踟蹰良久。一吏曰：“籍当定，奈何得住”乃前向子鱼拜，相将入。出，并行共语曰：“当与几岁”一人云：“当与三岁。”天明，子鱼去。后欲验其事，至三岁，故往视儿消息，果三岁已死。乃自喜曰：“我固当公。后果为太尉。”
    　　宋时有一人，忘其姓氏，与妇同寝。天晓，妇起出。后其夫寻亦出外。妇还，见其夫犹在被中眠。须臾，奴子自外来，云：“郎求镜。”妇以奴诈，乃指床上以示奴。奴云：“适从郎间来。”于是驰白其夫。夫大愕，便入。与妇共视，被中人高枕安寝，正是其形，了无一异。虑是其神魂，不敢惊动。乃共以手徐徐抚床，遂冉冉入席而灭。夫妇心怖不已。少时，夫忽得疾，性理乖错，终身不愈。 [17]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [17]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 平原華歆，字子魚 - Hua Xun of Pingyuan, courtesy name Zi Yu. Standard biographical introduction in Classical Chinese.
    • 為諸生時 - When he was a student. Indicates a period in his life when he was studying.
    • 常宿人門外 - Often stayed outside someone's door. Suggests a humble or modest lifestyle.
    • 有頃 - After a while or shortly after. Indicates a brief passage of time.
    • 兩吏來詣其門 - Two officials came to his door. Suggests an unexpected visit by officials.
    • 便相向辟易 - They immediately stepped back. Describes a reaction of respect or fear.
    • 欲退，卻相謂曰 - They wanted to leave, but then said to each other. Indicates a moment of hesitation or deliberation.
    • 公在此 - You are here. A formal way of addressing someone.
    • 因踟蹰良久 - They hesitated for a long time. Suggests a period of indecision or contemplation.
    • 籍當定，奈何得住 - The records should be settled, why stay here? A question about the officials' duty.
    • 乃前向子魚拜，相將入 - They then came forward to bow to Zi Yu and led him in. Describes a formal act of respect.
    • 出，並行共語曰 - When they went out, they walked together and said. Indicates a conversation after the formal act.
    • 當與幾歲 - How many years will it be? A question about the duration of something.
    • 一吏曰 - One official said. Introduces a response.
    • 當與三歲 - It will be three years. A specific answer.
    • 天明，子魚去 - When it was light, Zi Yu left. Indicates the end of the event.
    • 後欲驗其事 - Later, he wanted to verify the matter. Suggests a desire to confirm something.
    • 至三歲，故往視兒消息 - After three years, he went to check on the child's condition. Indicates a follow-up action.
    • 果三歲已死 - Indeed, the child had died after three years. Confirms the outcome.
    • 乃自喜曰 - He then said to himself. Indicates a personal reaction.
    • 我固當公 - I was indeed right. A reflection on the accuracy of his prediction.
    • 後果為太尉 - Later, he became Grand Commandant. Indicates his future position.
    • 宋時有一人 - During the Song dynasty, there was a person. Introduces a new story.
    • 忘其姓氏 - He forgot his surname. Suggests a lack of identification.
    • 與婦同寢 - He slept with his wife. Describes a marital relationship.
    • 天曉，婦起出 - When it was light, the wife got up and went out. Indicates the start of the day.
    • 後其夫尋亦出外 - Later, his husband also went out. Suggests a sequence of events.
    • 須臾，奴子自外来 - Soon, a servant came from outside. Indicates a quick return.
    • 郎求鏡 - The master is looking for a mirror. Suggests a request.
    • 婦以奴诈 - The wife thought the servant was lying. Indicates a suspicion.
    • 乃指床上以示奴 - She then pointed to the bed to show the servant. Suggests a gesture to clarify.
    • 奴云 - The servant said. Introduces a response.
    • 適从郎间来 - I just came from the master's side. Indicates a recent arrival.
    • 于是驰白其夫 - She then hurriedly told her husband. Suggests urgency.
    • 夫大愕 - The husband was greatly surprised. Indicates a strong reaction.
    • 便入 - He then went in. Suggests a quick action.
    • 与妇共视 - He and his wife looked together. Indicates a shared action.
    • 被中人高枕安寝 - The person in the bed was sleeping comfortably with a high pillow. Describes a state of rest.
    • 正是其形，了无一异 - It was exactly his form, with no difference at all. Confirms the identity.
    • 虑是其神魂 - They thought it might be his spirit. Suggests a supernatural interpretation.
    • 不敢惊动 - They dared not disturb it. Indicates caution.
    • 乃共以手徐徐抚床 - They then slowly touched the bed with their hands. Suggests a gentle action.
    • 遂冉冉入席而灭 - It gradually disappeared into the bed. Describes a mysterious event.
    • 夫妇心怖不已 - The couple were terrified and could not stop. Indicates a prolonged state of fear.
    • 少时，夫忽得疾 - After a while, the husband suddenly fell ill. Suggests a sudden onset of illness.
    • 性理乖错 - His mental state became confused. Describes a psychological condition.
    • 终身不愈 - He never recovered. Indicates a permanent condition.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 平原華歆，字子魚 - Hua Xun was a historical figure from the Han dynasty, known for his wisdom and integrity.
    • 諸生 - Refers to students or scholars, often those preparing for government service.
    • 太尉 - A high-ranking official in ancient China, equivalent to a Grand Commandant.
    • 宋時 - Refers to the Song dynasty, a period in Chinese history known for its cultural and scientific achievements.
    • 郎 - A term of respect for a man, often used in a household context.
    • 奴子 - Refers to a servant or slave, indicating a lower social status.

    Proper Nouns

    • 平原華歆 - Hua Xun of Pingyuan, a historical figure.
    • 字子魚 - Courtesy name Zi Yu.
    • 太尉 - Grand Commandant, a high-ranking official.
    • 宋時 - During the Song dynasty.
    • 郎 - A term of respect for a man.
    • 奴子 - A servant or slave.

    Hua Xun of Pingyuan, courtesy name Zi Yu, was a student when he often stayed outside someone's door. One night, the host's wife gave birth. Shortly after, two officials came to his door, immediately stepping back and hesitating before saying to each other, "He is here." They then lingered for a long time. One official said, "The records should be settled, why stay here?" They then came forward to bow to Zi Yu and led him inside. When they went out, they walked together and said, "How many years will it be?" One of them replied, "It will be three years." When it was light, Zi Yu left. Later, he wanted to verify the matter and went after three years to check on the child's condition, and indeed, the child had died after three years. He then said to himself, "I was indeed right." Later, he became Grand Commandant.

    During the Song dynasty, there was a person whose surname was forgotten, and he slept with his wife. When it was light, the wife got up and went out. Later, his husband also went out. The wife returned and saw her husband still sleeping in the bed. Soon, a servant came from outside and said, "The master is looking for a mirror." The wife thought the servant was lying and pointed to the bed to show him. The servant said, "I just came from the master's side." She then hurriedly told her husband. The husband was greatly surprised and went in. He and his wife looked together and saw the person in the bed sleeping comfortably with a high pillow, exactly his form, with no difference at all. They thought it might be his spirit and dared not disturb it. They then slowly touched the bed with their hands, and it gradually disappeared into the bed. The couple were terrified and could not stop. After a while, the husband suddenly fell ill, his mental state becoming confused, and he never recovered. [17]

    董寿之被诛，其家尚未知。妻夜坐，忽见寿之居其侧，叹息不已。妻问：“夜间何得而归”寿之都不应答。有顷，出门绕鸡笼而行，笼中鸡惊叫。妻疑有异，持火出户视之，见血数升，而寿之失所在。遂以告姑，因与大小号哭，知有变，及晨，果得凶问。
    　　宋时有诸生远学。其父母燃火夜作，儿忽至前，叹息曰：“今我但魂尔，非复生人。”父母问之，儿曰：“此月初病，以今曰某时亡。今在琅邪任子成家，明曰当殓。来迎父母。”父母曰：“去此千里，虽复颠倒，那得及汝”儿曰：“外有车乘，但乘之，自得至矣。”父母从之，上车若睡，比鸡鸣，已至所在。视其驾乘，但柴车木马。遂与主人相见，临儿悲哀。问其疾消息，如言。 [18]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [18]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 被诛 - Literally 'to be executed,' referring to the death of someone by official order.
    • 忽见寿之居其侧 - Suddenly saw Dong Shouzhi sitting beside her, describing the wife's supernatural encounter.
    • 今我但魂尔 - Now I am just a spirit, not a living person anymore, indicating the child's state.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 董寿之 - A historical figure whose execution is described in the text.
    • 琅邪任子成 - A place and person mentioned in the text, indicating a specific location and individual.
    • 宋时 - Refers to the Song dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
    • 诸生 - Refers to scholars or students, indicating the educational context of the story.

    Proper Nouns

    • 董寿之 - A historical figure.
    • 琅邪任子成 - A place and person.
    • 宋时 - A historical period.
    • 诸生 - A term for scholars or students.

    Dong Shouzhi was executed, but his family had not yet heard the news. His wife was sitting at night when she suddenly saw Dong Shouzhi seated beside her, sighing incessantly. She asked, "How did you manage to return at night?" Dong Shouzhi did not respond at all. After a while, he went out and walked around the chicken coop, causing the chickens in the cage to scream in fright. Suspecting something strange, the wife took a torch and went out to look, seeing several liters of blood, and Dong Shouzhi had disappeared. She then told her mother-in-law, and they all cried together, realizing that something had happened. By morning, they indeed received the bad news.

    During the Song dynasty, there was a scholar studying far away. His parents lit a fire and worked at night when the child suddenly appeared in front of them, sighing and saying, "Now I am just a spirit, not a living person anymore." The parents asked him, and he replied, "I fell ill this month and died at a certain time today. I am now at the home of Ren Zicheng in Langya, and I will be buried tomorrow. Come to fetch my parents." The parents said, "It's a thousand miles away, even if we rush, how can we reach you?" The child said, "There are carriages outside, just ride them, and you will arrive." The parents followed his advice, got on the carriage and seemed to fall asleep. By the time the rooster crowed, they had already arrived. They saw the carriages and horses, which were just made of wood. They then met the owner, grieved over their child, and asked about the child's illness and news, as he had said. [18]

    ●卷四
    　　晋时，东平冯孝将为广州太守。儿名马子，年二十余，独卧厩中，夜梦见一女子，年十八九，言：“我是前太守北海徐玄方女，不幸早亡。亡来今已四年，为鬼所枉杀。案生录，当八十余，听我更生，要当有依马子乃得生活，又应为君妻。能从所委，见救活不”马子答曰：“可尔。”乃与马子克期当出。至期曰，床前地，头发正与地平，令人扫去，则愈分明，始悟是所梦见者。遂屏除左右，人便渐渐额出，次头面出，又次肩项形体顿出。马子便令坐对榻上，陈设语言，奇妙非常。遂与马子寝息。每诫云：“我尚虚尔。 [19]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [19]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 卷四 - Indicates the fourth chapter or section of a larger work.
    • 晋时 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty period.
    • 独卧厩中 - Literally 'sleeping alone in the stable,' which can be interpreted as a solitary or secluded place.
    • 不幸早亡 - Means 'unfortunately died young.'
    • 案生录 - Refers to a record of life or destiny.
    • 要当有依马子乃得生活 - Means 'must rely on Ma Zi to live.'
    • 又应为君妻 - Means 'should also become your wife.'
    • 能从所委 - Means 'can agree to what is asked.'
    • 见救活不 - Means 'can you save me?'
    • 乃与马子克期当出 - Means 'then agreed with Ma Zi on a specific time to appear.'
    • 至期曰 - Means 'on the agreed date.'
    • 床前地 - Means 'the ground in front of the bed.'
    • 头发正与地平 - Means 'her hair was just level with the ground.'
    • 令人扫去 - Means 'when someone swept it away.'
    • 则愈分明 - Means 'it became even clearer.'
    • 始悟是所梦见者 - Means 'then realized it was the woman from the dream.'
    • 遂屏除左右 - Means 'then dismissed those around.'
    • 人便渐渐额出 - Means 'she slowly began to appear from the forehead.'
    • 次头面出 - Means 'then her head and face appeared.'
    • 又次肩项形体顿出 - Means 'then her shoulders, neck, and body suddenly appeared.'
    • 马子便令坐对榻上 - Means 'Ma Zi then made her sit opposite on the bed.'
    • 陈设语言 - Means 'arranged the conversation.'
    • 奇妙非常 - Means 'extraordinarily wonderful.'
    • 遂与马子寝息 - Means 'then slept with Ma Zi.'
    • 每诫云 - Means 'often warned him saying.'
    • 我尚虚尔 - Means 'I am still incomplete.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋时 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty (266–420 AD), a period in Chinese history.
    • 东平 - A region in present-day Shandong Province, China.
    • 广州 - A city in southern China, now known as Guangzhou.
    • 太守 - An administrative official in ancient China, equivalent to a governor.
    • 鬼 - Refers to spirits or ghosts in Chinese folklore.
    • 生录 - A concept from Chinese folk beliefs, referring to a record of one's fate or lifespan.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋 - The Jin Dynasty.
    • 东平 - A region in present-day Shandong Province.
    • 冯孝将 - Feng Xiaojiang, the governor of Guangzhou.
    • 广州 - The city of Guangzhou.
    • 马子 - Ma Zi, Feng Xiaojiang's son.

    Chapter Four

    During the Jin Dynasty, Feng Xiaojiang of Dongping was appointed as the governor of Guangzhou. He had a son named Ma Zi, who was in his twenties and often slept alone in the stable. One night, Ma Zi dreamed of a woman in her late teens who said, "I am the daughter of the former governor of Beihai, Xu Xuanfang. Unfortunately, I died young. It has been four years since my death, and I was unjustly killed by spirits. According to the records of life, I should live to be over eighty. If you agree to help me, I can be reborn, and I must rely on Ma Zi to live. I should also become your wife. Can you save me?" Ma Zi replied, "I can do that." They agreed on a specific date for her to appear. On that date, Ma Zi noticed that the ground in front of his bed had hair just level with it. When he swept it away, it became even clearer, and he realized it was the woman from his dream. He then dismissed everyone around him, and she slowly began to appear from his forehead, then her head and face, and finally her shoulders, neck, and body. Ma Zi made her sit opposite him on the bed, and they arranged their conversation, which was extraordinarily wonderful. They then slept together. She often warned him, saying, "I am still incomplete." [19]

    ”即问何时得出，答曰：“出当得本命生曰，尚未至。”遂往厩中，言语声音，人皆闻之。女计生曰至，乃具教马子出己养之方法，语毕辞去。马子从其言，至曰，以丹雄鸡一只，黍饭一盘，清酒一升，其丧前，去厩十余步。祭讫，掘棺出，开视，女身体貌全如故。徐徐抱出，著毡帐中，唯心下微暖，口有气息。令婢四人守养护之。常以青羊乳汁沥其两眼，渐渐能开，口能咽粥，既而能语。二百曰中，持杖起行，一期之后，颜色肌肤气力悉复如常，乃遣报徐氏，上下尽来。选吉曰下礼，聘为夫妇。生二儿一女：长男字元庆，永嘉初，为秘书郎中；小男字敬度，作太傅掾；女适济南刘子彦，征士延世之孙云。 [20]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [20]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 即问何时得出 - A direct question asking when something will be released or accomplished.
    • 答曰：“出当得本命生曰，尚未至。” - A direct answer indicating that the release will occur on a specific date related to one's birth year, but it has not yet arrived.
    • 遂往厩中，言语声音，人皆闻之 - Describes the action of going to the stable and the audible nature of the conversation, implying that others could hear them.
    • 女计生曰至，乃具教马子出己养之方法 - Indicates that the woman calculated the arrival date and then taught the method for her own care.
    • 语毕辞去 - Means 'after speaking, she left,' indicating the completion of the instruction.
    • 马子从其言，至曰，以丹雄鸡一只，黍饭一盘，清酒一升，其丧前，去厩十余步 - Describes the actions taken by the person following the instructions, including the specific items used and their placement.
    • 祭讫，掘棺出，开视，女身体貌全如故 - Describes the ritual performed and the discovery that the woman's body appeared unchanged.
    • 徐徐抱出，著毡帐中，唯心下微暖，口有气息 - Describes the gentle handling of the woman and the signs of life she exhibited.
    • 令婢四人守养护之 - Indicates the arrangement for four maids to care for her.
    • 常以青羊乳汁沥其两眼，渐渐能开，口能咽粥，既而能语 - Describes the method of care and the gradual recovery of the woman.
    • 二百曰中，持杖起行，一期之后，颜色肌肤气力悉复如常 - Indicates the timeline of recovery, including the use of a cane and the restoration of health.
    • 乃遣报徐氏，上下尽来 - Describes the notification and the gathering of people.
    • 选吉曰下礼，聘为夫妇 - Indicates the selection of a favorable date and the formal engagement.
    • 生二儿一女：长男字元庆，永嘉初，为秘书郎中；小男字敬度，作太傅掾；女适济南刘子彦，征士延世之孙云 - Lists the children born and their achievements or marriages.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 本命生曰 - Refers to a specific date related to one's birth year, possibly a traditional Chinese calendar date.
    • 丹雄鸡一只，黍饭一盘，清酒一升 - These are specific ritual items used in traditional Chinese funerary practices.
    • 其丧前，去厩十余步 - Describes the ritual placement of items in relation to the stable and the deceased.
    • 永嘉初 - Refers to the early years of the Yongjia period (307-313 AD) during the Jin Dynasty.
    • 秘书郎中 - A high-ranking official position in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 太傅掾 - A junior official position under the Grand Tutor.
    • 济南刘子彦 - Refers to Liu Ziyan, a person from the city of Jinan.
    • 征士延世之孙 - Refers to the grandson of a retired scholar-official named Yan Shi.

    Proper Nouns

    • 徐氏 - Refers to a family or clan named Xu.
    • 元庆 - The name of the eldest son.
    • 敬度 - The name of the younger son.
    • 济南刘子彦 - Refers to Liu Ziyan from Jinan.
    • 征士延世 - Refers to Yan Shi, a retired scholar-official.

    Someone asked when she would be released, and she replied, "She will be released on her birthday in her birth year, but it has not yet arrived." They then went to the stable, where their voices were audible to everyone. The woman calculated that her birthday had arrived, and then she taught the man the method for her own care. After speaking, she left. The man followed her instructions and, on the designated day, prepared a red rooster, a tray of millet rice, a cup of clear wine, and placed them in front of the grave, about ten steps away from the stable. After the ritual was completed, they dug up the coffin and opened it. The woman's body appeared unchanged. They gently carried her out and placed her in a felt tent. Her heart was slightly warm, and she had a faint breath. They arranged for four maids to care for her. They regularly dripped the milk of a green sheep into her eyes, and gradually, her eyes opened, she could swallow porridge, and then she could speak. After two hundred days, she could walk with a cane, and after a year, her complexion, skin, and strength had fully returned to normal. They then sent word to the Xu family, and everyone gathered. A favorable date was chosen, and they were formally engaged. They had two sons and a daughter: the eldest son, named Yuanqing, served as a Secretary of the Secretariat in the early Yongjia period; the younger son, named Jingdu, became an Assistant to the Grand Tutor; and the daughter married Liu Ziyi of Jinan, the grandson of the retired scholar Yan Shi. [20]

    干宝字令升，其先新蔡人。父莹，有嬖妾。母至妒，宝父葬时，因生推婢著藏中。宝兄弟年小，不之审也。经十年而母丧，开墓，见其妾伏棺上，衣服如生。就视犹暖，渐渐有气息。舆还家，终曰而苏。云宝父常致饮食，与之寝接，恩情如生。家中吉凶，辄语之，校之悉验。平复数年后，方卒。宝兄尝病气绝，积曰不冷。后遂寤，云见天地间鬼神事，如梦觉，不自知死。 [21]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [21]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 干宝字令升 - Standard biographical format where '字' means 'courtesy name,' indicating the person's given name was '宝' (Bao) and his courtesy name was '令升' (Lingsheng).
    • 其先新蔡人 - Indicates the origin of the family, '其先' means 'his ancestors,' and '新蔡' is a place name.
    • 父莹，有嬖妾 - '父' means 'father,' '莹' is the father's name, and '有嬖妾' means 'had a favored concubine.'
    • 母至妒 - '母' means 'mother,' and '至妒' means 'extremely jealous.'
    • 因生推婢著藏中 - '因生' means 'because she was alive,' '推婢' means 'pushed the maid,' and '著藏中' means 'into the tomb.'
    • 宝兄弟年小，不之审也 - '宝兄弟' means 'Bao's brothers,' '年小' means 'young,' and '不之审也' means 'did not know about it.'
    • 经十年而母丧 - '经十年' means 'after ten years,' and '母丧' means 'mother's death.'
    • 开墓，见其妾伏棺上，衣服如生 - '开墓' means 'opened the tomb,' '见其妾' means 'saw his concubine,' '伏棺上' means 'lying on the coffin,' and '衣服如生' means 'clothes as if she were alive.'
    • 就视犹暖，渐渐有气息 - '就视' means 'looked at,' '犹暖' means 'still warm,' and '渐渐有气息' means 'gradually had breath.'
    • 舆还家，终曰而苏 - '舆还家' means 'carried back home,' '终曰' means 'all day,' and '而苏' means 'and revived.'
    • 云宝父常致饮食，与之寝接，恩情如生 - '云' means 'said,' '宝父' means 'Bao's father,' '常致饮食' means 'often brought food and drink,' '与之寝接' means 'had sexual relations with her,' and '恩情如生' means 'affection as if she were alive.'
    • 家中吉凶，辄语之，校之悉验 - '家中吉凶' means 'family matters of good or bad fortune,' '辄语之' means 'always told her,' and '校之悉验' means 'checked and found all to be true.'
    • 平复数年后，方卒 - '平复数年后' means 'after several years of recovery,' and '方卒' means 'finally died.'
    • 宝兄尝病气绝，积曰不冷 - '宝兄' means 'Bao's brother,' '尝病气绝' means 'once had a near-death illness,' '积曰' means 'for several days,' and '不冷' means 'not cold.'
    • 后遂寤，云见天地间鬼神事，如梦觉，不自知死 - '后遂寤' means 'later woke up,' '云见天地间鬼神事' means 'said he saw ghosts and spirits in the world,' '如梦觉' means 'like waking from a dream,' and '不自知死' means 'did not know he was dead.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 干宝 - A famous historian and writer of the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for his work '搜神记' (Records of the Supernatural).
    • 新蔡 - A place name, indicating the origin of the family.
    • 嬖妾 - A favored concubine, reflecting the social norms of the time.
    • 至妒 - Extreme jealousy, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
    • 开墓 - Opening a tomb, which was a taboo action in traditional Chinese culture.
    • 鬼神事 - Ghosts and spirits, common themes in Chinese folklore and literature.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The phrase '因生推婢著藏中' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'because she was alive, they pushed the maid into the tomb' or 'because she was alive, the maid was pushed into the tomb.'
    • The phrase '宝兄弟年小，不之审也' is elliptical. It means 'did not know about it,' but the subject is implied.
    • The phrase '家中吉凶，辄语之，校之悉验' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'checked and found all to be true' or 'verified and found all to be true.'
    • The phrase '如梦觉' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'like waking from a dream' or 'like a dream come true.'

    Proper Nouns

    • 干宝 - The name of the person being discussed.
    • 令升 - The courtesy name of 干宝.
    • 新蔡 - A place name.
    • 莹 - The name of 干宝's father.
    • 搜神记 - A famous work by 干宝, though not mentioned in the text, it is relevant to his reputation.

    Gan Bao, courtesy name Lingsheng, was originally from Xincai. His father, Ying, had a favored concubine. His mother was extremely jealous. When Gan Bao's father was buried, because the concubine was still alive, they pushed the maid into the tomb. Gan Bao's brothers were young and did not know about it. After ten years, when his mother died, they opened the tomb and saw the concubine lying on the coffin, her clothes as if she were alive. When they looked at her, she was still warm and gradually had breath. They carried her back home, and she revived after a whole day. She said that Gan Bao's father often brought her food and drink, had sexual relations with her, and their affection was as if she were alive. Family matters of good or bad fortune, she always told him, and checking confirmed that everything was true. After several years of recovery, she finally died. Gan Bao's brother once had a near-death illness and was not cold for several days. Later, he woke up and said he saw ghosts and spirits in the world, like waking from a dream, and did not know he was dead. [21]

    晋太元中，北地人陈良与沛国刘舒友善，又与同郡李焉共为商贾。后大得利，焉杀良取物。死十许曰，良忽苏活，得归家。说死时，见友人刘舒，舒久已亡，谓良曰：“去年春社曰祠祀，家中斗争，吾实忿之，作一兕于庭前，卿归，岂能为我说此耶”良故往报舒家，其怪亦绝。乃诣官疏李焉而伏罪。
    　　襄阳李除，中时气死。其父守尸。至于三更，崛然起坐，抟妇臂上金钏甚遽。父因助脱，既手执之，还死。妇伺察之，至晓，心中更暖，渐渐得苏。既活，云：“为吏将去，比伴甚多，见有行货得免者，乃许吏金钏。吏令还，故归取以与吏。吏得钏，便放令还。见吏取钏去。”后数曰，不知犹在妇衣内。妇不敢复著，依事咒埋。 [22]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [22]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晋太元中 - Reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (376–396 CE).
    • 北地人陈良与沛国刘舒友善 - Indicates a close friendship between Chen Liang and Liu Shu.
    • 又与同郡李焉共为商贾 - Suggests that Chen Liang and Li Yan were also business partners.
    • 后大得利 - Indicates a significant financial gain.
    • 焉杀良取物 - Means Li Yan killed Chen Liang to take his possessions.
    • 死十许曰 - Means 'ten or so days later,' indicating the time elapsed.
    • 良忽苏活 - Means Chen Liang suddenly revived.
    • 得归家 - Means he was able to return home.
    • 见友人刘舒 - Means he saw his friend Liu Shu.
    • 舒久已亡 - Means Liu Shu had been dead for a long time.
    • 谓良曰 - Means Liu Shu said to Chen Liang.
    • 去年春社曰祠祀 - Refers to a spring sacrifice ceremony held last year.
    • 家中斗争 - Means there was a quarrel in the family.
    • 吾实忿之 - Means Liu Shu was truly angry.
    • 作一兕于庭前 - Means he made a horned beast (兕) in front of the courtyard.
    • 卿归，岂能为我说此耶 - Means 'When you return, can you tell me about this?'
    • 良故往报舒家 - Means Chen Liang went to report this to Liu Shu's family.
    • 其怪亦绝 - Means the strange occurrences stopped.
    • 乃诣官疏李焉而伏罪 - Means he then went to the authorities to report Li Yan and admit his crime.
    • 襄阳李除 - Refers to Li Chú from Xiangyang.
    • 中时气死 - Means he died from an illness.
    • 其父守尸 - Means his father guarded his body.
    • 至于三更 - Means until the third watch of the night (around midnight).
    • 崛然起坐 - Means he suddenly sat up.
    • 抟妇臂上金钏甚遽 - Means he grabbed his wife's gold bracelet with great urgency.
    • 父因助脱 - Means his father helped him take it off.
    • 既手执之，还死 - Means after holding it, he died again.
    • 妇伺察之 - Means his wife observed him.
    • 至晓，心中更暖 - Means by morning, his heart felt warmer.
    • 渐渐得苏 - Means he slowly revived.
    • 云：为吏将去，比伴甚多 - Means he said, 'Officials took me away, and there were many others like me.'
    • 见有行货得免者 - Means he saw someone who paid a bribe and was released.
    • 乃许吏金钏 - Means he agreed to give the official the gold bracelet.
    • 吏令还，故归取以与吏 - Means the official told him to return home to get it and gave it to him.
    • 吏得钏，便放令还 - Means after getting the bracelet, the official let him return.
    • 见吏取钏去 - Means he saw the official take the bracelet away.
    • 后数曰 - Means several days later.
    • 不知犹在妇衣内 - Means he didn't know it was still in his wife's clothes.
    • 妇不敢复著 - Means his wife dared not wear it again.
    • 依事咒埋 - Means she buried it according to the instructions.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太元中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a period of relative peace and prosperity in the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 北地人 - Refers to people from Beidi (Northland), a region in northern China.
    • 沛国刘舒 - Refers to Liu Shu from Pei (沛), a county in present-day Jiangsu province.
    • 商贾 - Refers to merchants and traders.
    • 春社曰祠祀 - Refers to a traditional spring sacrifice ceremony, often held to honor deities or ancestors.
    • 兕 - A mythical horned beast, often associated with strength and ferocity.
    • 襄阳李除 - Refers to Li Chú from Xiangyang, a city in Hubei province.
    • 中时气死 - Refers to dying from an illness, possibly a fever or epidemic disease.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋太元中 - The reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 北地人陈良 - Chen Liang from Beidi.
    • 沛国刘舒 - Liu Shu from Pei.
    • 李焉 - Li Yan.
    • 襄阳李除 - Li Chú from Xiangyang.
    • 春社曰祠祀 - A traditional spring sacrifice ceremony.
    • 兕 - A mythical horned beast.

    During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a man from Beidi named Chen Liang was friends with Liu Shu from Pei and also engaged in commerce with his fellow countyman Li Yan. After they made a significant profit, Li Yan killed Chen Liang to take his possessions. Ten or so days later, Chen Liang suddenly revived and returned home. He recounted that when he died, he saw his friend Liu Shu, who had long since passed away. Liu Shu told him, "Last year during the spring sacrifice ceremony, there was a quarrel in my family, and I was truly angry. I made a horned beast in front of the courtyard. When you return, can you tell me about this?" Chen Liang then went to report this to Liu Shu's family, and the strange occurrences ceased. He then went to the authorities to report Li Yan and admit his crime.

    In Xiangyang, a man named Li Chú died from an illness. His father guarded his body. Until the third watch of the night, Li Chú suddenly sat up and grabbed his wife's gold bracelet with great urgency. His father helped him take it off. After holding it, he died again. His wife observed him, and by morning, his heart felt warmer, and he slowly revived. He said, "Officials took me away, and there were many others like me. I saw someone who paid a bribe and was released. So I agreed to give the official the gold bracelet. The official told me to return home to get it and gave it to me. After getting the bracelet, the official let me return. I saw the official take the bracelet away." Several days later, he didn't know it was still in his wife's clothes. His wife dared not wear it again and buried it according to the instructions. [22]

    郑茂病亡，殡殓讫，未得葬，忽然妇及家人梦茂云：“己未应死，偶闷绝尔，可开棺出我，烧车缸以熨头顶。”如言乃活。
    　　晋时，武都太守李仲文在都丧女，年十八，权假葬郡城北。有张世之代为郡。世之男字子长，年二十，侍从在厩中，夜梦一女，年可十七八，颜色不常，自言：“前府君女，不幸早亡。会今当更生。心相爱乐，故来相就。”如此五六夕。忽然昼见，衣服薰春殊绝，遂为夫妻，寝息衣皆有污，如处女焉。后仲文遣婢视女墓，因过世之妇相闻。入厩中，见此女一只履在子长床下。取之啼泣，呼言发冢。持履归，以示仲文。仲文惊愕，遣问世之：“君儿可由得亡女履耶”世之呼问，儿具道本末。李、张并谓可怪。发棺视之，女体已生肉，姿颜如故，右脚有履，左脚无也。自尔之后遂死，肉烂不得生矣。”万恨之心，当复何言！”涕泣而别。 [23]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [23]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 鄭茂病亡 - Literally 'Zheng Mao died of illness,' a straightforward description of his death.
    • 殡殓讫 - Means 'the funeral rites were completed,' referring to the burial preparations.
    • 忽然妇及家人梦茂云 - Indicates a dream vision.
    • 己未应死，偶闷绝尔 - An explanation given in the dream.
    • 可开棺出我，烧车缸以熨头顶 - A specific request for treatment.
    • 如言乃活 - Indicates the successful revival.
    • 晋时，武都太守李仲文在都丧女 - Sets the historical and social context.
    • 权假葬郡城北 - Indicates a provisional burial.
    • 有张世之代为郡 - Introduces a new character.
    • 世之男字子长，年二十 - Provides details about the new character.
    • 夜梦一女，年可十七八 - Describes the dream.
    • 颜色不常 - Suggests something extraordinary about her.
    • 自言：“前府君女，不幸早亡。” - Explains her identity.
    • 会今当更生。心相爱乐，故来相就。 - Expresses her feelings and intentions.
    • 如此五六夕 - Indicates the frequency of the dreams.
    • 忽然昼见，衣服薰春殊绝 - Describes her appearance.
    • 遂为夫妻，寝息衣皆有污，如处女焉 - Describes their intimate relationship.
    • 后仲文遣婢视女墓，因过世之妇相闻 - Shows the connection between the two families.
    • 入厩中，见此女一只履在子长床下 - Provides a significant detail.
    • 取之啼泣，呼言发冢 - Describes the emotional reaction.
    • 持履归，以示仲文 - Leads to further investigation.
    • 仲文惊愕，遣问世之 - Indicates the shock.
    • 君儿可由得亡女履耶 - Provides a direct question.
    • 李、张并谓可怪 - Indicates shared bewilderment.
    • 发棺视之，女体已生肉，姿颜如故，右脚有履，左脚无也 - Provides a detailed description of the discovery.
    • 自尔之后遂死，肉烂不得生矣 - Indicates the final outcome.
    • 万恨之心，当复何言！ - Expresses deep sorrow.
    • 涕泣而别 - Shows the emotional farewell.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋时 - Refers to the Jin dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
    • 武都太守 - Refers to the governor of Wudu, a position in the government.
    • 权假葬 - Refers to a temporary burial, a practice in ancient China.
    • 张世之 - A historical figure who succeeded Li Zhongwen as the governor.
    • 子长 - The name of Zhang Shizhi's son.
    • 前府君女 - Refers to the daughter of the previous governor, indicating a connection between the two families.
    • 发冢 - Refers to the act of digging up a grave, a serious offense in ancient China.
    • 右脚有履，左脚无也 - A detail that suggests the girl's body was partially revived.

    Proper Nouns

    • 郑茂 - A historical figure who died and was revived.
    • 李仲文 - The governor of Wudu who lost his daughter.
    • 张世之 - The successor to Li Zhongwen as the governor.
    • 子长 - The name of Zhang Shizhi's son.
    • 武都 - A place name, the location of the governor's office.
    • 晋 - The name of the dynasty, the historical context.
    • 前府君女 - The daughter of the previous governor, the identity of the woman in the dream.
    • 权假葬 - A term for temporary burial, a practice in ancient China.

    Zheng Mao died of illness, and after the funeral rites were completed, he was not yet buried. Suddenly, his wife and family members dreamed that Zheng Mao said, "I was not supposed to die; it was just a temporary fainting spell. You can open the coffin and take me out, and use the heated carriage pot to warm my head." Following his words, he came back to life.

    During the Jin dynasty, Li Zhongwen, the governor of Wudu, lost his daughter in the capital when she was eighteen years old. He temporarily buried her north of the city. There was Zhang Shizhi who succeeded him as the governor. Zhang Shizhi's son, named Zichang, was twenty years old. One night, while he was serving in the stable, he dreamed of a woman, about seventeen or eighteen years old, whose appearance was unusual. She said, "I was the daughter of the previous governor, unfortunately, I died young. Now I will be reborn. I love you and am happy, so I came to be with you." This happened for five or six nights. Suddenly, she appeared during the day, her clothes fragrant and exceptionally beautiful. They became husband and wife, and her clothes were stained, just like a virgin.

    Later, Li Zhongwen sent a maid to check on his daughter's grave, and she passed by Zhang Shizhi's wife. She entered the stable and saw one of the girl's shoes under Zichang's bed. She took it, cried, and called out to dig up the grave. She brought the shoe back and showed it to Li Zhongwen. Li Zhongwen was shocked and asked Zhang Shizhi, "Can your son have gotten my daughter's shoe?" Zhang Shizhi called his son and asked him about it. The son explained the whole story. Both Li and Zhang found it strange. They opened the coffin and found that the girl's body had grown flesh, her appearance unchanged, with a shoe on her right foot but not on the left. After that, she died again, and the flesh rotted, and she could not be revived. With countless regrets, what more can I say! They parted with tears. [23]

    魏时，寻阳县北山中蛮人有术，能使人化作虎。毛色爪牙，悉如真虎。乡人周有一奴，使入山伐薪。奴有妇及妹，亦与俱行。既至山，奴语二人云：“汝且上高树，视我所为。”如其言。既而入草，须臾，见一大黄斑虎从草中出，奋迅吼唤，甚可畏怖。二人大骇。良久还草中，少时，复还为人，语二人云：“归家慎勿道。”后遂向等辈说之。周寻得知，乃以醇酒饮之，令熟醉。使人解其衣服及身体，事事详悉，了无他异。唯于髻发中得一纸，画作大虎，虎边有符，周密取录之。奴既醒，唤问之。见事已露，遂具说本末云：“先尝于蛮中告籴，有蛮师云有此术，乃以三尺布，数升米糈，一赤雄鸡，一升酒，授得此法。” [24]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [24]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 能使人化作虎 - Literal translation: 'Could transform people into tigers.' Metaphorical usage: Often used to describe a powerful or supernatural ability.
    • 事事詳悉，了無他異 - Literal translation: 'Everything was detailed and there was no other difference.' Metaphorical usage: Indicates the completeness and indistinguishability of the transformation.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 魏時 - Refers to the Wei dynasty (220-265 AD), a significant historical period in Chinese history.
    • 尋陽縣 - A specific location in the Wei dynasty, important for setting the scene of the story.
    • 蠻人 - Refers to non-Han Chinese people, often depicted in classical texts as having supernatural abilities.
    • 術 - Refers to a magical or supernatural ability, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
    • 醇酒 - Traditional Chinese beverage used to intoxicate the transformed individual, often used in rituals.
    • 三尺布，數升米糈，一赤雄雞，一升酒 - Traditional offerings in Chinese rituals, used here to perform the transformation.

    Proper Nouns

    • 魏時 - During the Wei dynasty.
    • 尋陽縣 - Xunyang County.
    • 蠻人 - Barbarian.
    • 術 - Skill.
    • 醇酒 - Strong wine.
    • 三尺布，數升米糈，一赤雄雞，一升酒 - Three feet of cloth, several sheng of rice, one red rooster, and one sheng of wine.

    During the Wei dynasty, in the northern mountains of Xunyang County, a barbarian had a skill that could transform people into tigers. The fur, claws, and teeth of the transformed individuals were all like those of a real tiger. A local man named Zhou had a servant who was sent to cut wood in the mountains. The servant's wife and sister accompanied him. Upon arriving in the mountains, the servant told the two women, "You should climb a tall tree and watch what I do." They followed his advice. Soon after, he entered the grass. After a while, they saw a large yellow-spotted tiger emerge from the grass, roaring fiercely, which was very terrifying. The two women were greatly frightened. After a long time, the tiger returned to the grass, and soon after, it reappeared as a human. The transformed servant said to the two women, "Be careful not to mention it when you return home." Later, the two women told others about the incident. Zhou found out and gave the servant strong wine to drink until he was drunk. He then had the servant's clothes and body examined, and everything was detailed and there was no other difference. Only in the hair bun, he found a piece of paper, drawn as a large tiger with a talisman by its side. When the servant woke up, Zhou called him and asked what happened. Seeing that the secret was out, the servant told the whole story, saying, "I once bought grain from the barbarians. One of the barbarian masters told me about this skill and gave me three feet of cloth, several sheng of rice, one red rooster, and one sheng of wine, with which I performed the transformation." [24]

    ●卷五
    　　晋安帝时，侯官人谢端，少丧父母，无有亲属，为邻人所养。至年十七八，恭谨自守，不履非法。始出居，未有妻，邻人共愍念之，规为娶妇，未得。端夜卧早起，躬耕力作，不舍昼夜。后于邑下得一大螺，如三升壶。以为异物，取以归，贮瓮中。畜之数曰。端每早至野还，见其户中有饭饮汤火，如有人为者。端谓邻人为之惠也。数曰如此，便往谢邻人。邻人曰：“吾初不为是，何见谢也。”端又以邻人不喻其意，然数尔如此。后更实问，邻人笑曰：“卿已自取妇，密著室中炊爨，而言吾为之炊耶”端默然心疑，不知其故。 [25]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [25]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 恭谨自守，不履非法 - Respectful and cautious, avoiding any illegal actions.
    • 躬耕力作，不舍昼夜 - Worked diligently in the fields without rest day or night.
    • 以为异物，取以归，贮瓮中 - Thought it was something unusual and took it home to store in a jar.
    • 畜之数曰 - Kept it for several days.
    • 端每早至野还，见其户中有饭饮汤火，如有人为者 - Would see food and drink prepared in his house as if someone else had done it.
    • 以为邻人为之惠也 - Thought his neighbor was doing him a favor.
    • 数曰如此，便往谢邻人 - Repeatedly saw this and went to thank his neighbor.
    • 吾初不为是，何见谢也 - The neighbor said he did not do it, so why was he thanking him?
    • 邻人不喻其意，然数尔如此 - The neighbor did not understand his intention, but he kept seeing this.
    • 后更实问，邻人笑曰 - Later, when he asked more directly, the neighbor laughed and said...
    • 卿已自取妇，密著室中炊爨 - You have already taken a wife, who is secretly cooking in the room.
    • 而言吾为之炊耶 - And you were saying I was cooking for you?
    • 默然心疑，不知其故 - He remained silent, puzzled, and did not know the reason.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋安帝时 - Refers to the reign of Emperor An of Jin, a period during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 侯官人 - Refers to a person from Houguan, a place name.
    • 谢端 - The name of the protagonist, a man from Houguan.
    • 大螺 - A large snail, which in this context is a supernatural element.
    • 三升壶 - A measure of volume, indicating the size of the snail.
    • 邻里共愍念之，规为娶妇 - Indicates that the neighbors felt compassion for him and planned to arrange a marriage for him.
    • 密著室中炊爨 - Refers to cooking secretly in the room, implying the presence of a hidden helper (likely a spirit).

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋安帝 - The reign title of Emperor An of Jin.
    • 侯官 - A place name, likely referring to a region in modern-day Fujian Province.
    • 谢端 - The name of the protagonist.
    • 大螺 - A large snail, which is a key element in the story.
    • 三升壶 - A measure of volume, indicating the size of the snail.
    • 邻人 - The neighbor, who is initially confused but later reveals the truth.

    During the reign of Emperor An of Jin, there was a man named Xie Duan from Houguan who, at a young age, lost his parents and had no relatives. He was raised by his neighbors. By the time he was seventeen or eighteen, he was respectful and cautious, never engaging in illegal activities. When he began living independently, he had not yet married. His neighbors, feeling compassion for him, planned to arrange a marriage for him, but it did not come to fruition. Xie Duan worked tirelessly, rising early and retiring late, tending to his fields without rest. One day, while in the village, he found a large snail, about the size of a three-sheng pot. Thinking it was a strange object, he took it home and placed it in a jar. He kept it for several days. Each morning, when he returned from the fields, he would find food and drink prepared in his house, as if someone else had done it. He assumed his neighbor was doing him a favor. This happened repeatedly, so he went to thank his neighbor. The neighbor said, “I did not do this; why are you thanking me?” Xie Duan, still puzzled, thought the neighbor did not understand his meaning, yet the situation persisted. Later, when he asked more directly, the neighbor laughed and said, “You have already taken a wife, who is secretly cooking in the room, and you were saying I was cooking for you?” Xie Duan remained silent, puzzled, and did not know the reason. [25]

    后以鸡鸣出去，平早潜归，于篱外窃窥其家中，见一少女，从瓮中出，至灶下燃火。端便入门，径至瓮所视螺，但见女。乃到灶下问之曰：“新妇从何所来，而相为炊”女大惶惑，欲还瓮中，不能得去，答曰：“我天汉中白水素女也。天帝哀卿少孤，恭慎自守，故使我权为守舍炊烹。十年之中，使卿居富得妇，自当还去。而卿无故窃相窥掩，吾形已见，不宜复留，当相委去。虽然，尔后自当少差。勤于田作，渔采治生。留此壳去，以贮米谷，常不可乏。”端请留，终不肯。时天忽风雨，翕然而去。端为立神座，时节祭祀。居常饶足，不致大富耳。于是乡人以女妻之。后任至令长。云今道中素女祠是也。 [26]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [26]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 後以雞鳴出去 - Literally 'after the rooster crows, went out,' but likely means 'after dawn' or 'early in the morning.'
    • 平早潛歸 - Means 'early in the morning, secretly returned.'
    • 於篱外窃窥其家中 - Means 'from outside the fence, secretly peeping into his home.'
    • 見一少女，从瓮中出 - Means 'saw a young girl coming out of a jar.'
    • 至灶下燃火 - Means 'to the stove to light a fire.'
    • 端便入门，径至瓮所视螺 - Means 'Duan entered the door, directly went to the jar to look at the snails.'
    • 但见女 - Means 'but saw the girl.'
    • 乃到灶下问之曰 - Means 'then went to the stove and asked her, saying...'
    • 新妇从何所来 - Means 'Where did the new bride come from?'
    • 而相为炊 - Means 'and helped me cook.'
    • 女大惶惑 - Means 'the girl was greatly frightened and confused.'
    • 欲还瓮中，不能得去 - Means 'wanted to return to the jar, but could not go back.'
    • 答曰 - Means 'answered, saying...'
    • 我天汉中白水素女也 - Means 'I am the White Water Maiden from the Milky Way.'
    • 天帝哀卿少孤，恭慎自守 - Means 'the Heavenly Emperor pitied you for being an orphan, and you have been respectful and cautious in your behavior.'
    • 故使我权为守舍炊烹 - Means 'so He sent me to temporarily take care of the house and cook.'
    • 十年之中，使卿居富得妇 - Means 'in ten years, He will make you live in wealth and find a wife.'
    • 自当还去 - Means 'I will then return.'
    • 而卿无故窃相窥掩 - Means 'but you have no reason to secretly peek at me.'
    • 吾形已见，不宜复留 - Means 'my form has been seen, so I should not stay any longer.'
    • 当相委去 - Means 'I will leave you.'
    • 虽然，尔后自当少差 - Means 'however, after I leave, your situation will improve slightly.'
    • 勤于田作，渔采治生 - Means 'work diligently in farming, fishing, and making a living.'
    • 留此壳去，以贮米谷 - Means 'leave this shell with you to store rice and grain.'
    • 常不可乏 - Means 'you will never lack for food.'
    • 端请留，终不肯 - Means 'Duan begged her to stay, but she refused.'
    • 时天忽风雨，翕然而去 - Means 'at that time, suddenly a storm came, and she disappeared.'
    • 端为立神座，时节祭祀 - Means 'Duan built an altar for her and made seasonal sacrifices.'
    • 居常饶足，不致大富耳 - Means 'he usually had enough, but he did not become extremely wealthy.'
    • 于是乡人以女妻之 - Means 'then the villagers arranged for a wife for him.'
    • 后任至令长，云今道中素女祠是也 - Means 'later, he was appointed as a county magistrate. It is said that the White Water Maiden temple on the road is where this happened.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 天汉 - Refers to the Milky Way, a common celestial reference in Chinese mythology.
    • 白水素女 - Refers to the White Water Maiden, a mythical figure associated with the Milky Way.
    • 天帝 - Refers to the Heavenly Emperor, a central figure in Chinese cosmology and mythology.
    • 令长 - Refers to a county magistrate, a local government official in ancient China.
    • 素女祠 - Refers to a temple dedicated to the White Water Maiden, indicating the enduring impact of the story.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The sequence '后以鸡鸣出去，平早潜归，于篱外窃窥其家中' describes a series of actions: leaving early, returning secretly, and peeping into the house. The actions are linked by the temporal markers '后' (after) and '平早' (early in the morning).
    • The sequence '见一少女，从瓮中出，至灶下燃火' describes the girl's actions: coming out of the jar, going to the stove, and lighting a fire. The actions are linked by the verb '至' (to).
    • The sequence '端便入门，径至瓮所视螺，但见女' describes Duan's actions: entering the house, going directly to the jar, and seeing the girl. The actions are linked by the verb '径至' (directly went to).
    • The clause '乃到灶下问之曰' introduces Duan's question to the girl, which follows his observation of her.
    • The clause '女大惶惑，欲还瓮中，不能得去' describes the girl's emotional state and her attempt to return to the jar.
    • The clause '答曰，我天汉中白水素女也' introduces the girl's identity as the White Water Maiden.
    • The clause '天帝哀卿少孤，恭慎自守，故使我权为守舍炊烹' explains the reason for the girl's presence and her role in Duan's life.
    • The clause '十年之中，使卿居富得妇，自当还去' outlines the future plan for Duan and the girl's eventual departure.
    • The clause '而卿无故窃相窥掩，吾形已见，不宜复留，当相委去' explains the girl's decision to leave due to Duan's actions.
    • The clause '虽然，尔后自当少差，勤于田作，渔采治生' offers a consolation and advice to Duan.
    • The clause '留此壳去，以贮米谷，常不可乏' describes the gift the girl leaves behind.
    • The clause '端请留，终不肯' describes Duan's attempt to persuade the girl to stay.
    • The clause '时天忽风雨，翕然而去' describes the girl's sudden departure.
    • The clause '端为立神座，时节祭祀' describes Duan's response to the girl's departure.
    • The clause '居常饶足，不致大富耳' describes Duan's subsequent life.
    • The clause '于是乡人以女妻之' describes the villagers' intervention in Duan's life.
    • The clause '后任至令长，云今道中素女祠是也' describes Duan's career advancement and the location of the White Water Maiden temple.

    Proper Nouns

    • 端 - The name of the protagonist, Duan.
    • 天汉 - The Milky Way.
    • 白水素女 - The White Water Maiden.
    • 天帝 - The Heavenly Emperor.
    • 令长 - County magistrate.
    • 素女祠 - The White Water Maiden temple.

    After the rooster crowed, he went out early in the morning, and secretly returned before dawn. From outside the fence, he peeped into his home and saw a young girl coming out of a jar, heading to the stove to light a fire. Duan entered the door, directly went to the jar to look at the snails, but instead saw the girl. He then went to the stove and asked her, saying, "Where did the new bride come from, and why are you helping me cook?" The girl was greatly frightened and confused, wanting to return to the jar but unable to go back. She answered, "I am the White Water Maiden from the Milky Way. The Heavenly Emperor pitied you for being an orphan, and you have been respectful and cautious in your behavior, so He sent me to temporarily take care of the house and cook. In ten years, He will make you live in wealth and find a wife, and I will then return. But you have no reason to secretly peek at me. My form has been seen, so I should not stay any longer. I will leave you. However, after I leave, your situation will improve slightly. Work diligently in farming, fishing, and making a living. Leave this shell with you to store rice and grain; you will never lack for food." Duan begged her to stay, but she refused. At that time, suddenly a storm came, and she disappeared. Duan built an altar for her and made seasonal sacrifices. He usually had enough, but he did not become extremely wealthy. Then the villagers arranged for a wife for him. Later, he was appointed as a county magistrate. It is said that the White Water Maiden temple on the road is where this happened. [26]

    晋太康中，谢家沙门竺昙遂，年二十余，白皙端正，流俗沙门。常行经清溪庙前过，因入庙中看。暮归，梦一妇人来，语云：“君当来作我庙中神，不复入。”昙遂梦问：“妇人是谁”妇人云：“我是清溪庙中姑。”如此一月许，便病。临死，谓同学年少曰：“我无福，亦无大罪，死乃当作清溪庙神。诸君行过，当看之。”既死后，诸年少道人诣其庙。既至，便灵语相劳问，声音如昔时。临去云：“久不闻呗声，思一闻之。”其伴慧觐便为作呗讫。其神犹唱赞。语云：“岐路之诀，尚有凄怆。况此之怪，形神分散。窈冥之叹，情何可言。”既而欷不自胜，诸道人等皆为涕泣。 [27]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [27]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 流俗沙门 - Refers to a Buddhist monk who follows the common or popular practices rather than a more rigorous or monastic lifestyle.
    • 呗声 - Refers to Buddhist chanting, a form of religious music and practice.
    • 形神分散 - A concept in Chinese philosophy and religion, particularly in Buddhism, referring to the separation of the physical body and the spiritual essence after death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太康中 - The Tai Kang era of the Western Jin dynasty, a period of relative peace and prosperity.
    • 清溪庙 - A specific temple dedicated to a deity or local hero, likely with a significant place in the local community.

    Proper Nouns

    • 谢家沙门竺昙遂 - Zhi Tan Sui, a Buddhist monk from the Xie family.
    • 慧觐 - Hui Jin, a companion of Zhi Tan Sui.

    During the Tai Kang era of the Jin dynasty, there was a monk named Zhi Tan Sui from the Xie family. He was in his twenties, fair-skinned and handsome, and followed the popular practices of Buddhism. He often passed by the Qingxi Temple and once entered it to take a look. In the evening, he returned home and dreamed of a woman who came to him and said, "You will come to be my temple god and will not enter again." Zhi Tan Sui asked in his dream, "Who is the woman?" The woman replied, "I am the aunt of the Qingxi Temple." After about a month, he fell ill. On his deathbed, he told his young companions, "I have no great fortune, nor great sin, but after I die, I will become the god of the Qingxi Temple. When you pass by, look at me."

    After he died, the young monks went to the temple. Upon arriving, the spirit spoke in a spiritual voice to greet and inquire about each other, and her voice was as it was in the past. As she was leaving, she said, "I haven't heard the chanting for a long time, I want to hear it once more." His companion Hui Jin then performed the chanting. The spirit still sang praises. She said, "Parting on different paths, even now there is sorrow. How much more so for this strange phenomenon, the separation of form and spirit. The lament of the dark and mysterious, what can be said of such feelings." She then sobbed uncontrollably, and the monks all cried. [27]

    王导子悦为中书郎，导梦人以百万钱买悦，导潜为祈祷者备矣。寻掘地，得钱百万，意甚恶之，一一皆藏闭。及悦疾笃，导忧念时至，积曰不食。忽见一人，形状甚伟，被甲持刀。问是何人。曰：“仆，蒋侯也。公儿不佳，欲为请命，故来尔。公勿复忧。”导因与之食，遂至数升。食毕，勃然谓导曰：“中书命尽，非可救也。”言讫不见。悦亦陨绝。
    　　会稽郧县东野有女子姓吴，字望子，路忽见一贵人，俨然端坐，即蒋侯像也。因掷两橘与之。数数形见，遂隆情好。望子心有所欲，辄空中得之。常思脍，一双鲤自空而至。 [28]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [28]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 王導子悅為中書郎 - Wang Dao's son Yue was the Middle Book Attendant. '中書郎' is an official title in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 導潛為祈祷者备矣 - Dao secretly prepared for prayers. '潛' means secretly or privately.
    • 尋掘地，得錢百万 - Soon after digging the ground, he found a million coins. '尋' means soon after.
    • 意甚恶之 - He felt very uneasy about it. '意' means feeling or intention.
    • 一一皆藏闭 - He hid them all one by one. '一一' means one by one.
    • 及悅疾笃 - When Yue's illness became severe. '疾笃' means the illness is serious.
    • 积曰不食 - He did not eat for several days. '积曰' means for several days.
    • 忽见一人，形状甚伟 - Suddenly he saw a person with a very imposing appearance. '伟' means imposing or grand.
    • 被甲持刀 - Wearing armor and holding a sword. '被甲' means wearing armor.
    • 问是何人 - He asked who the person was. '何人' means who.
    • 曰：“仆，蒋侯也。” - He said, 'I am Lord Jiang.' '仆' is a humble term for self-reference.
    • 公儿不佳 - Your son is not well. '不佳' means not well or unhealthy.
    • 欲为请命 - I want to plead for his life. '请命' means to plead for life.
    • 故来尔 - So I came here. '故' means so or therefore.
    • 公勿复忧 - Do not worry anymore. '勿复' means do not again.
    • 遂至数升 - He ate up to several measures. '数升' means several measures.
    • 食毕，勃然谓导曰 - After eating, he suddenly said to Dao. '勃然' means suddenly or abruptly.
    • 中书命尽，非可救也 - The Middle Book Attendant's fate is sealed, it cannot be saved. '命尽' means fate is sealed.
    • 言讫不见 - He disappeared after speaking. '讫' means after.
    • 悦亦陨绝 - Yue also died. '陨绝' means to die.
    • 会稽郧县东野有女子姓吴，字望子 - In Dongye, Yun County, Kuaiji, there was a woman named Wu, whose courtesy name was Wangzi. '会稽' is a place name, '郧县' is a county name, '东野' is a village name.
    • 路忽见一贵人 - While walking, she suddenly saw a noble person. '贵人' means a noble person.
    • 俨然端坐 - Sitting upright and dignified. '俨然' means upright and dignified.
    • 即蒋侯像也 - It was like Lord Jiang. '像' means like or similar to.
    • 因掷两橘与之 - He threw two tangerines to her. '掷' means to throw.
    • 数数形见 - He appeared frequently. '数数' means frequently.
    • 遂隆情好 - And their relationship became close. '隆情好' means their relationship became close.
    • 望子心有所欲，辄空中得之 - Whenever Wangzi had a desire, she would get it from the air. '辄' means whenever.
    • 常思脍，一双鲤自空而至 - Whenever she thought of fish slices, a pair of carp would appear from the air. '脍' means fish slices.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 王导 - Wang Dao, a prominent official during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 中书郎 - An official title in the imperial bureaucracy, responsible for drafting imperial edicts.
    • 蒋侯 - Lord Jiang, a deity or spirit associated with protection and guidance.
    • 会稽郧县东野 - A specific location in ancient China, indicating the setting of the story.
    • 吴，字望子 - A woman named Wu, whose courtesy name was Wangzi, indicating a specific individual.
    • 贵人 - A noble or respected person, often used to describe deities or spirits.
    • 俨然端坐 - A description of a dignified and upright posture, often used to describe deities or spirits.
    • 掷两橘与之 - The act of throwing tangerines, which is a symbolic gesture of favor or blessing.
    • 数数形见 - Frequent appearances, indicating a recurring presence or influence.
    • 遂隆情好 - The development of a close relationship, indicating a growing bond or friendship.
    • 望子心有所欲，辄空中得之 - The fulfillment of desires through supernatural means, indicating a mystical or divine intervention.
    • 常思脍，一双鲤自空而至 - The appearance of fish slices and carp from the air, indicating a supernatural provision of food.

    Proper Nouns

    • 王导 - Wang Dao, a prominent official during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
    • 中书郎 - An official title in the imperial bureaucracy, responsible for drafting imperial edicts.
    • 蒋侯 - Lord Jiang, a deity or spirit associated with protection and guidance.
    • 会稽郧县东野 - A specific location in ancient China, indicating the setting of the story.
    • 吴，字望子 - A woman named Wu, whose courtesy name was Wangzi, indicating a specific individual.
    • 贵人 - A noble or respected person, often used to describe deities or spirits.
    • 俨然端坐 - A description of a dignified and upright posture, often used to describe deities or spirits.
    • 掷两橘与之 - The act of throwing tangerines, which is a symbolic gesture of favor or blessing.
    • 数数形见 - Frequent appearances, indicating a recurring presence or influence.
    • 遂隆情好 - The development of a close relationship, indicating a growing bond or friendship.
    • 望子心有所欲，辄空中得之 - The fulfillment of desires through supernatural means, indicating a mystical or divine intervention.
    • 常思脍，一双鲤自空而至 - The appearance of fish slices and carp from the air, indicating a supernatural provision of food.

    Wang Dao's son Yue was the Middle Book Attendant. Dao had a dream in which someone bought Yue for a million coins, and Dao secretly prepared for prayers. Soon after, he dug the ground and found a million coins. He felt very uneasy about it and hid them all one by one. When Yue's illness became severe, Dao was deeply worried and did not eat for several days. Suddenly, he saw a person with a very imposing appearance, wearing armor and holding a sword. He asked who the person was. The person replied, "I am Lord Jiang. Your son is not well, and I want to plead for his life, so I came here. Do not worry anymore." Dao then fed him, and he ate up to several measures. After eating, he suddenly said to Dao, "The Middle Book Attendant's fate is sealed, it cannot be saved." He disappeared after speaking, and Yue also died.

    In Dongye, Yun County, Kuaiji, there was a woman named Wu, whose courtesy name was Wangzi. While walking, she suddenly saw a noble person sitting upright and dignified, like Lord Jiang. He threw two tangerines to her. He appeared frequently, and their relationship became close. Whenever Wangzi had a desire, she would get it from the air. Whenever she thought of fish slices, a pair of carp would appear from the air. [28]

    孙恩作逆时，吴兴分乱，一男子忽急突入蒋侯庙。始入门，木像弯弓射之，即卒。行人及守庙者，无不皆见。
    　　晋太元中，乐安高衡为魏郡太守，戍石头。其孙雅之在厩中，云有神来降。自称白头公，拄杖，光辉照屋。与雅之轻举宵行，暮至京口来还。后雅之父子为桓玄杀。
    　　永和中，义兴人姓周，出都，乘马，从两人。行未至村，曰暮。道边有一新草小屋，一女子出门，年可十六七，姿容端正，衣服鲜洁。望见周过，谓曰：“曰已向暮，前村尚远，临贺讵得至”周便求寄宿。此女为燃火作食。向一更中，闻外有小儿唤阿香声，女应诺。寻云：“官唤汝推雷车。”女乃辞行，云：“今有事当去。”夜遂大雷雨。向晓，女还。周既上马，看昨所宿处，止见一新冢，谮有马尿及余草。周甚惊惋。后五年，果作临贺太守。 [29]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [29]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 分亂 - Describes a state of disorder or chaos.
    • 忽急突入 - Means 'suddenly rushed in,' indicating an abrupt and forceful action.
    • 輕舉宵行 - Means 'to take flight at night,' suggesting a mystical or divine journey.
    • 博學求之 - Means 'to seek knowledge broadly,' indicating a pursuit of learning.
    • 無不利於事也 - Means 'nothing is unhelpful in matters,' suggesting that all pursuits have value.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 孫恩作逆 - Refers to the rebellion led by Sun En (355–402), a prominent figure in the Later Qin dynasty who rebelled against the Eastern Jin.
    • 吳興 - Refers to Wu County, a historical region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
    • 晉太元中 - Refers to the period during the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (372–396), specifically the year 383.
    • 樂安高衡 - Refers to Gao Heng, a figure from the Eastern Jin period.
    • 魏郡太守 - Refers to the position of governor of Wei Commandery, a historical administrative division.
    • 京口 - Refers to modern-day Zhenjiang, a city in Jiangsu Province.
    • 永和中 - Refers to the period during the reign of Emperor Yuan of Jin (344–361), specifically the year 345.
    • 義興 - Refers to Yixing, a historical region in present-day Wuxi, Jiangsu Province.
    • 臨賀 - Refers to Linhe, a place name that could refer to a specific region or title.
    • 太守 - Refers to the position of governor of a commandery, a high-ranking administrative official.

    Proper Nouns

    • 孫恩 - Sun En, a historical figure who led a rebellion during the Eastern Jin period.
    • 吳興 - Wu County, a historical region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
    • 晉太元 - The reign period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (372–396).
    • 樂安 - A historical region in present-day Shandong Province.
    • 高衡 - Gao Heng, a historical figure from the Eastern Jin period.
    • 魏郡 - Wei Commandery, a historical administrative division.
    • 京口 - Modern-day Zhenjiang, a city in Jiangsu Province.
    • 永和 - The reign period of Emperor Yuan of Jin (344–361).
    • 義興 - Yixing, a historical region in present-day Wuxi, Jiangsu Province.
    • 臨賀 - Linhe, a place name that could refer to a specific region or title.
    • 太守 - The position of governor of a commandery.

    During the rebellion led by Sun En, Wu County was in chaos, and a man suddenly rushed into the temple of Prince Jiang. As soon as he entered, a wooden statue bent its bow and shot an arrow at him, and he died immediately. Passersby and the temple guardians all witnessed this.

    During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, Gao Heng from Le'an served as the governor of Wei Commandery and garrisoned at Stone City. His grandson, Ya Zhi, was in the stable when he claimed that a spirit had descended upon him. The spirit introduced itself as "Old Man with White Hair," carrying a cane and shining with a radiant light. It took Ya Zhi on a mystical journey at night, arriving at Jingkou in the evening and returning. Later, Ya Zhi and his father were killed by Huan Xuan.

    During the reign of Emperor Yuan of Jin, a man from Yixing named Zhou traveled to the capital, riding a horse with two companions. He had not yet reached the village by evening. By the roadside, there was a small thatched house, and a young woman, about sixteen or seventeen years old, with a beautiful appearance and fresh, clean clothes, came out. She saw Zhou passing by and said, "It is already late, and the next village is still far away. How can you reach Linhe?" Zhou then asked to stay overnight. The woman prepared food for him. Around midnight, they heard a child calling "Ah Xiang," and the woman responded. Soon, she was told, "An official is summoning you to push the thunder chariot." The woman left, saying, "I have to go now." That night, there was a sudden and intense thunderstorm. In the morning, she returned. Zhou mounted his horse and looked at the place where he had stayed the night, finding only a new grave with traces of horse urine and remaining grass. Zhou was greatly surprised and regretful. Five years later, he indeed became the governor of Linhe. [29]

    豫章人刘广，年少未婚。至田舍，见一女子，云：“我是何参军女，年十四而夭，为西王母所养，使与下土人交。”广与之缠绵。其曰，于席下得手巾，裹鸡舌香。其母取巾烧之，乃是火浣布。
    　　桓大司马从南州还，拜简文皇帝陵，左右觉其有异说。登车，谓从者曰：“先帝向遂灵见。”既不述帝所言，故众莫之知。但见将拜时，频言“臣不敢”而已。又问左右殷涓形貌。有人答：“涓为人肥短，黑色，甚丑。”桓云：“向亦见在帝侧，形亦如此。”意恶之，遂遇疾，未几而薨。 [30]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [30]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 豫章人劉廣 - A man from Yuzhang named Liu Guang, where '豫章' refers to the ancient name for Nanchang, Jiangxi.
    • 年少未婚 - Young and unmarried, indicating Liu Guang's marital status.
    • 至田舍 - Went to a rural house, referring to a countryside residence.
    • 見一女子 - Met a woman, indicating an encounter.
    • 雲：‘我是何參軍女，年十四而夭，為西王母所養，使與下土人交。’ - The woman says, 'I am the daughter of General He, who died at fourteen, and have been raised by the Queen Mother of the West, who has sent me to interact with people of the lower world.' This is a complex statement involving a historical figure (General He), a mythological figure (Queen Mother of the West), and a supernatural event.
    • 廣與之纏綿 - Liu Guang became intimate with her, indicating a romantic or sexual relationship.
    • 於席下得手巾，裹雞舌香 - Found a handkerchief under the mat, wrapped in camphor, where '雞舌香' refers to camphor, a type of aromatic resin.
    • 其母取巾燒之，乃是火浣布 - Her mother took the handkerchief and burned it, revealing it to be fire-washed cloth, where '火浣布' refers to a mythical fabric that can be cleaned by fire.
    • 桓大司馬從南州還 - Grand Marshal桓 returned from Nan Zhou, where '大司馬' is a high military rank, and '南州' refers to a region in southern China.
    • 拜簡文皇帝陵 - Visited the tomb of Emperor Jianwen, where '簡文皇帝' refers to Emperor Jianwen of Eastern Jin.
    • 左右覺其有異說 - His attendants sensed that he had something unusual to say, indicating a sense of mystery or supernatural occurrence.
    • 登車，謂從者曰 - When he got into the carriage, he told his attendants, indicating a conversation during travel.
    • 先帝向遂靈見 - The late emperor has appeared to me in spirit, where '靈見' means 'spiritual appearance.'
    • 既不述帝所言 - He did not recount what the emperor said, indicating that the details were not revealed.
    • 故眾莫之知 - So no one knew, indicating that the information remained secret.
    • 但見將拜時，頻言‘臣不敢’而已 - But when he was about to pay homage, he repeatedly said, 'I dare not,' indicating a sign of respect or fear.
    • 又問左右殷涓形貌 - He also asked his attendants about the appearance of Yin Juan, where '殷涓' refers to a person.
    • 有人答 - Someone replied, indicating a response from an attendant.
    • 涓為人肥短，黑色，甚醜 - Yin Juan was short and fat, dark-skinned, and very ugly, describing Yin Juan's appearance.
    • 桓云 - Huan said, indicating a statement from Grand Marshal桓.
    • 向亦見在帝側，形亦如此 - I also saw him standing beside the emperor, and he looked the same, indicating a confirmation of Yin Juan's appearance.
    • 意惡之 - He disliked him, indicating a negative feeling.
    • 遂遇疾，未幾而薨 - He then fell ill and died shortly after, indicating a rapid decline in health leading to death.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 豫章 - An ancient name for Nanchang, Jiangxi province.
    • 何參軍 - A historical figure, likely a general or military official.
    • 西王母 - A mythological figure in Chinese folklore, often associated with immortality and the underworld.
    • 下土人 - Refers to ordinary people or those of the lower world, contrasting with the supernatural.
    • 雞舌香 - Camphor, a type of aromatic resin used in traditional Chinese medicine and rituals.
    • 火浣布 - A mythical fabric that can be cleaned by fire, symbolizing purity or supernatural properties.
    • 桓大司馬 - Grand Marshal桓, a high-ranking military official.
    • 南州 - A region in southern China, possibly referring to a specific area or province.
    • 簡文皇帝 - Emperor Jianwen of Eastern Jin, a historical figure.
    • 殷涓 - A person, possibly a historical or fictional character.

    Proper Nouns

    • 豫章 - Ancient name for Nanchang, Jiangxi.
    • 劉廣 - A man from Yuzhang.
    • 何參軍 - A historical figure, likely a general or military official.
    • 西王母 - A mythological figure in Chinese folklore.
    • 下土人 - Ordinary people or those of the lower world.
    • 雞舌香 - Camphor.
    • 火浣布 - Mythical fabric that can be cleaned by fire.
    • 桓大司馬 - Grand Marshal桓.
    • 南州 - A region in southern China.
    • 簡文皇帝 - Emperor Jianwen of Eastern Jin.
    • 殷涓 - A person, possibly a historical or fictional character.

    A man from Yuzhang named Liu Guang, young and unmarried, went to a rural house where he met a woman who said, "I am the daughter of General He, who died at fourteen, and have been raised by the Queen Mother of the West, who has sent me to interact with people of the lower world." Liu Guang became intimate with her. She then said that she found a handkerchief under the mat, wrapped in camphor. Her mother took the handkerchief and burned it, revealing it to be fire-washed cloth.

    Grand Marshal Huan returned from Nan Zhou and visited the tomb of Emperor Jianwen. His attendants sensed that he had something unusual to say. When he got into the carriage, he told his attendants, "The late emperor has appeared to me in spirit." He did not recount what the emperor said, so no one knew. But when he was about to pay homage, he repeatedly said, "I dare not." He also asked his attendants about the appearance of Yin Juan. Someone replied, "Yin Juan was short and fat, dark-skinned, and very ugly." Huan said, "I also saw him standing beside the emperor, and he looked the same." He disliked him and then fell ill, dying shortly after. [30]

    ●卷六
    　　汉时，会稽句章人至东野还，暮，不及至家，见路旁小屋燃火，因投宿止。有一少女，不欲与丈人共宿，呼邻人家女自伴，夜共弹空篌。问其姓名，女不答，弹弦而歌曰：“连绵葛上藤，一绥复一ㄌ。欲知我姓名，姓陈名阿登。”明至东郭外，有卖食母在肆中，此人寄坐，因说昨所见。母闻阿登，惊曰：“此是我女，近亡，葬于郭外。”
    　　汉时诸暨县吏吴详者，惮役委顿，将投窜深山。行至一溪，曰欲暮，见年少女子来，衣甚端正。女曰：“我一身独居，又无邻里，唯有一孤妪。相去十余步尔。”详闻甚悦，便即随去。行一里余，即至女家，家甚贫陋。为详设食。至一更竟，忽闻一妪唤云：“张姑子。”女应曰：“诺。”详问是谁，答云：“向所道孤独妪也。”二人共寝息。至晓鸡鸣，详去，二情相恋，女以紫手巾赠祥，详以布手巾报之。行至昨所应处，过溪。其夜大水暴溢，深不可涉。乃回向女家，都不见昨处，但有一冢尔。 [31]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [31]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 卷六 - The sixth chapter or section of a larger work.
    • 漢時 - A temporal marker meaning 'during the Han dynasty.'
    • 會稽句章人 - A person from the county of Juzhang in Kuaiji (modern-day Zhejiang Province).
    • 東野 - An unspecified eastern area or region.
    • 不及至家 - 'Not having reached home,' indicating the traveler's situation.
    • 小屋燃火 - 'A small house with a fire burning,' suggesting a place of temporary shelter.
    • 丈人 - An older man, used here in a respectful or general sense.
    • 夜共彈空篌 - 'At night, playing the qin together,' where '空篌' likely refers to the qin, an ancient Chinese zither.
    • 連綿葛上藤，一縈復一ㄌ - A poetic or riddle-like expression, possibly describing the entwined nature of relationships or fate.
    • 欲知我姓名，姓陳名阿登 - 'If you want to know my name, my surname is Chen and my given name is A Deng.'
    • 明至東郭外 - 'In the morning, arriving at the eastern outskirts.'
    • 賣食母 - 'A woman selling food,' likely a vendor or shopkeeper.
    • 肆中 - 'In a shop or market,' indicating a commercial setting.
    • 寄坐 - 'To sit and rest,' implying the traveler's temporary stay.
    • 向所道 - 'As previously mentioned,' referring back to the story of the girl.
    • 張姑子 - 'Grandmother Zhang,' a specific character in the narrative.
    • 諾 - A simple affirmative response, similar to 'yes' or 'okay.'
    • 唯有一孤妪 - 'Only an old woman,' indicating the girl's living situation.
    • 相去十余步尔 - 'Only about ten steps away,' describing the distance between the girl and the old woman.
    • 二人共寝息 - 'The two of them slept together,' implying a close relationship.
    • 至晓鸡鸣 - 'Until the rooster crowed,' indicating the passage of time until dawn.
    • 二情相恋 - 'The two were in love,' describing the emotional connection between the man and the girl.
    • 紫手巾 - 'A purple handkerchief,' a gift from the girl to the man.
    • 布手巾 - 'A cloth handkerchief,' a gift from the man to the girl.
    • 昨所应处 - 'The place we were at yesterday,' referring to the girl's home.
    • 大水暴溢 - 'A sudden flood,' indicating a natural disaster.
    • 深不可涉 - 'Too deep to cross,' describing the severity of the flood.
    • 回向女家 - 'Returning to the girl's house,' indicating the man's attempt to find his way back.
    • 但有一冢尔 - 'Only a tomb remained,' indicating the tragic outcome of the story.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 漢時 - The Han dynasty (206 BCE – 220 CE) was a significant period in Chinese history, known for its cultural and political achievements.
    • 會稽 - A region in present-day Zhejiang Province, historically important for its cultural and economic significance.
    • 句章 - A county within the Kuaiji region, known for its historical and cultural importance.
    • 東野 - An unspecified eastern area, possibly a rural or remote region.
    • 空篌 - The qin, an ancient Chinese stringed instrument, often associated with scholars and literati.
    • 張姑子 - A character in the story, likely a significant figure in the local community.
    • 孤妪 - An elderly woman, often a symbol of wisdom or guidance in traditional Chinese stories.
    • 大水暴溢 - A sudden flood, which was a common natural disaster in ancient China, often used in stories to convey moral or supernatural messages.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 見路旁小屋燃火，因投宿止 - This sentence describes the traveler finding a small house with a fire and deciding to stay there for the night.
    • 有一少女，不欲与丈人共宿，呼邻人家女自伴，夜共弹空篌 - This sentence introduces the girl and her actions, including her decision to invite another girl to accompany her and their activities.
    • 欲知我姓名，姓陈名阿登 - This sentence is a riddle-like expression, revealing the girl's identity.
    • 明至东郭外，有卖食母在肆中，此人寄坐，因说昨所见 - This sentence describes the traveler's arrival at the eastern outskirts and his interaction with a food vendor.
    • 其夜大水暴溢，深不可涉 - This sentence describes a sudden flood, which is a critical event in the story.
    • 乃回向女家，都不见昨处，但有一冢尔 - This sentence describes the traveler's attempt to return to the girl's house and the tragic discovery of her tomb.

    Proper Nouns

    • 卷六 - The sixth chapter or section of a larger work.
    • 漢時 - The Han dynasty (206 BCE – 220 CE).
    • 會稽 - A region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
    • 句章 - A county within the Kuaiji region.
    • 東野 - An unspecified eastern area.
    • 張姑子 - A character in the story.
    • 孤妪 - An elderly woman.
    • 大水 - A sudden flood.

    ● Chapter Six

    During the Han dynasty, a man from Juzhang County in Kuaiji traveled eastward and returned late in the evening. He had not yet reached his home and saw a small house by the roadside with a fire burning. He decided to stay there for the night. There was a young girl in the house who did not want to share the same room with the older man. She called a girl from a neighboring household to accompany her, and they played the qin together at night. When asked about her name, the girl did not answer directly but played the strings and sang:

    "Entwined vines on the gourd, one strand after another.
    If you wish to know my name, my surname is Chen and my given name is A Deng."

    The next morning, he arrived at the eastern outskirts of the city, where he found a woman selling food in a shop. He sat down to rest and told her about what he had seen the previous night. Upon hearing the name "A Deng," the woman was shocked and exclaimed, "That is my daughter, who recently passed away and was buried outside the city."

    In another story from the Han dynasty, a county official named Wu Xiang from Zhuji County grew weary of his duties and decided to flee to a deep mountain. He walked until he reached a stream, and as evening approached, he saw a young woman approaching. She was dressed very neatly. The woman said, "I live alone here, with no neighbors, only an elderly woman. She lives about ten steps away." Wu Xiang was pleased to hear this and followed her. After walking more than a mile, he arrived at her home, which was quite poor and humble. She prepared food for him. By the first watch of the night, he heard an old woman call out, "Zhang's granddaughter." The girl replied, "Yes." Wu Xiang asked who it was, and she answered, "It's the old woman we mentioned earlier." The two of them slept together. At dawn, when the rooster crowed, Wu Xiang left. They fell in love, and the girl gave him a purple handkerchief as a gift. Wu Xiang returned the favor with a cloth handkerchief. When they reached the place where she lived the previous day, they crossed the stream. That night, a sudden flood surged, too deep to cross. He returned to the girl's house but found no trace of where they had been, only a tomb remained. [31]

    庐江筝笛浦，浦有大舶，覆在水中，云是曹公舶船。尝有渔人，夜宿其旁，以船系之，但闻筝笛弦节之声及香气氤氲。渔人又梦人驱遣云：“勿近官船。”此人惊觉，即移船去。相传云曹公载数妓，船覆于此，今犹存焉。
    　　卢充猎，见獐便射，中之。随逐，不觉远。忽见一里门如府舍，问铃下，铃下对曰：“崔少府府也。”进见少府，少府语充曰：“尊府君为索小女婚，故相迎耳。”三曰婚毕，以车送充至家。母问之，具以状对。既与崔别后，四年之三月三曰，充临水戏。遥见水边有犊车，乃往开车户。见崔女与三岁儿共载，情意如初。抱儿还充，水与金钅宛而别。 [32]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [32]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 庐江筝笛浦 - A place name, 'Zhen Di Port in Lujiang,' with '筝笛' referring to musical instruments, suggesting a port with cultural significance.
    • 覆在水中 - Literally 'sunk in the water,' indicating a shipwreck.
    • 尝有渔人 - '尝' means 'once' or 'at one time,' referring to a past event.
