    • 付厨 - Means 'gave to the kitchen' or 'assigned to the kitchen.'
    • 旦且以二头作□，便著潘汁，□中养之 - Elliptical phrase requiring clarification: '□' likely refers to a dish or container. '作□' means 'to prepare' or 'to cook.' '著潘汁' means 'to pour on millet juice.' '□中养之' means 'to raise them in the dish.'
    • 其暮梦有十丈夫，并著乌布裤褶 - '其暮' means 'that night.' '十丈夫' means 'ten men.' '并著乌布裤褶' means 'all wearing black cloth trousers and jackets.'
    • 自反缚，向宗渊叩首，若求哀 - '自反缚' means 'bound themselves.' '向宗渊叩首' means 'bowing to Zong Yuan.' '若求哀' means 'as if pleading for mercy.'
    • 明旦，厨人宰二龟 - '明旦' means 'the next morning.' '厨人' means 'the cook.' '宰二龟' means 'slaughtered two turtles.'
    • 其暮复梦八人求哀如初 - '其暮' means 'that night.' '复梦' means 'had another dream.' '八人求哀如初' means 'eight men pleading for mercy as before.'
    • 宗渊方悟，令勿杀 - '宗渊方悟' means 'Zong Yuan finally realized.' '令勿杀' means 'ordered not to kill.'
    • 明夜还梦见八人来，跪谢恩 - '明夜还梦见' means 'the next night, he had another dream.' '八人来' means 'eight men came.' '跪谢恩' means 'kneeling to thank him.'
    • 遂不复食龟 - Means 'thereafter, he no longer ate turtle.'
    • 熊无穴，居大树孔中 - '熊无穴' means 'bears have no dens.' '居大树孔中' means 'live in the holes of big trees.'
    • 东土呼熊为子路 - '东土' means 'eastern lands.' '呼熊为子路' means 'call bears 'Zilu.'
    • 以物击树云：'子路可见。' - '以物击树' means 'hit the tree with an object.' '子路可见' means 'Zilu can be seen.'
    • 于是便下 - '于是' means 'then.' '便下' means 'they would descend.'
    • 不呼则不动也 - Means 'if not called, they do not move.'
    • 鄱阳县民黄赭，入山采荆杨子，遂迷不知道 - '鄱阳县民' means 'a resident of Poyang County.' '黄赭' is a person's name. '入山采荆杨子' means 'went into the mountains to collect *Platycladus orientalis* seeds.' '遂迷不知道' means 'became lost and did not know the way.'
    • 数曰，饥饿，忽见一大龟 - '数曰' means 'after several days.' '饥饿' means 'hungry.' '忽见一大龟' means 'suddenly saw a large turtle.'
    • 赭便咒曰：'汝是灵物，吾迷不知道，今骑汝背，示吾路。' - '赭便咒曰' means 'Zhang Zhe then cursed, saying.' '汝是灵物' means 'you are a divine creature.' '吾迷不知道' means 'I am lost and do not know the way.' '今骑汝背，示吾路' means 'now I will ride on your back and you will show me the way.'
    • 龟即回右转，赭即从行 - '龟即回右转' means 'the turtle immediately turned right.' '赭即从行' means 'Zhang Zhe followed.'
    • 去十馀里，便至溪水，见贾客行船 - '去十馀里' means 'after traveling more than ten miles.' '便至溪水' means 'they arrived at a stream.' '见贾客行船' means 'saw merchants sailing.'
    • 赭即往乞食，便语船人曰 - '赭即往乞食' means 'Zhang Zhe went to beg for food.' '便语船人曰' means 'then spoke to the boatmen.'
    • 我向者於溪边见一龟，甚大，可共往取之 - '我向者於溪边见一龟' means 'I saw a turtle by the stream earlier.' '甚大' means 'very large.' '可共往取之' means 'we can go together to catch it.'
    • 言讫，面即生疮 - '言讫' means 'after he finished speaking.' '面即生疮' means 'his face immediately broke out in sores.'
    • 既往，亦复不见龟 - '既往' means 'after going.' '亦复不见龟' means 'they also did not see the turtle.'
    • 还家数曰，病疮而死 - '还家数曰' means 'after returning home for several days.' '病疮而死' means 'died from the sores.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋太元 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (376–396 CE).
    • 寻阳 - An ancient name for Jiujiang, a city in Jiangxi Province.
    • 太守 - The title of a governor in the Han and Jin dynasties.
    • 龟 - Turtles were often associated with longevity and wisdom in Chinese culture.
    • 熊 - Bears were often associated with strength and ferocity in Chinese folklore.
    • 子路 - A historical figure and one of Confucius's disciples, known for his loyalty and bravery.
    • 鄱阳县 - A county in Jiangxi Province.
    • 荆杨子 - The seeds of *Platycladus orientalis* (Chinese arborvitae), used for medicinal purposes.

    Zong Yuan, courtesy name Shulin, was from Nanyang. During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, he served as the governor of Xunyang. He had dozens of turtles given to the kitchen, and ordered that two be prepared each morning by pouring millet juice over them and raising them in a dish. That night, he dreamed of ten men, all wearing black cloth trousers and jackets, who bound themselves and bowed to Zong Yuan, pleading for mercy. The next morning, the cook slaughtered two turtles. That night, he had another dream of eight men pleading for mercy as before. Zong Yuan finally realized what was happening and ordered that they not be killed. The next night, he had another dream of eight men coming to thank him. He woke up startled. The following morning, he released the turtles into Mount Lu, and thereafter, he no longer ate turtle.

    Bears have no dens and live in the holes of big trees. In the eastern lands, bears are called Zilu. When an object is hit against a tree and "Zilu can be seen" is said, then they would descend. If not called, they do not move.

    A resident of Poyang County named Huang Zhe went into the mountains to collect Platycladus orientalis seeds and became lost, not knowing the way. After several days, he was hungry and suddenly saw a large turtle. Huang Zhe then cursed, saying, "You are a divine creature. I am lost and do not know the way. Now I will ride on your back and you will show me the way." The turtle immediately turned right, and Huang Zhe followed. After traveling more than ten miles, they arrived at a stream where they saw merchants sailing. Huang Zhe went to beg for food and spoke to the boatmen, saying, "I saw a turtle by the stream earlier. It was very large. We can go together to catch it." After he finished speaking, his face immediately broke out in sores. After going, they also did not see the turtle. After returning home for several days, he died from the sores. [67]

    宗渊字叔林，南阳人。晋太元中，为寻阳太守，有数十头龟付厨，敕旦且以二头作□，便著潘汁，□中养之。其暮梦有十丈夫，并著乌布裤褶，自反缚，向宗渊叩首，若求哀。明旦，厨人宰二龟。其暮复梦八人求哀如初。宗渊方悟，令勿杀。明夜还梦见八人来，跪谢恩。於是惊觉。明朝自人庐山放之，遂不复食龟。
    　　熊无穴，居大树孔中。东土呼熊为子路。以物击树云：“子路可见。”於是便下。不呼则不动也。
    　　鄱阳县民黄赭，入山采荆杨子，遂迷不知道。数曰，饥饿，忽见一大龟，赭便咒曰：“汝是灵物，吾迷不知道，今骑汝背，示吾路。”龟即回右转，赭即从行。去十馀里，便至溪水，见贾客行船。赭即往乞食，便语船人曰：“我向者於溪边见一龟，甚大，可共往取之。”言讫，面即生疮。既往，亦复不见龟。还家数曰，病疮而死。

    Zong Yuan, courtesy name Shulin, was from Nanyang. During the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, he served as the governor of Xunyang. He had dozens of turtles given to the kitchen, and ordered that two be prepared each morning by pouring millet juice over them and raising them in a dish. That night, he dreamed of ten men, all wearing black cloth trousers and jackets, who bound themselves and bowed to Zong Yuan, pleading for mercy. The next morning, the cook slaughtered two turtles. That night, he had another dream of eight men pleading for mercy as before. Zong Yuan finally realized what was happening and ordered that they not be killed. The next night, he had another dream of eight men coming to thank him. He woke up startled. The following morning, he released the turtles into Mount Lu, and thereafter, he no longer ate turtle.

    Bears have no dens and live in the holes of big trees. In the eastern lands, bears are called Zilu. When an object is hit against a tree and "Zilu can be seen" is said, then they would descend. If not called, they do not move.

    A resident of Poyang County named Huang Zhe went into the mountains to collect Platycladus orientalis seeds and became lost, not knowing the way. After several days, he was hungry and suddenly saw a large turtle. Huang Zhe then cursed, saying, "You are a divine creature. I am lost and do not know the way. Now I will ride on your back and you will show me the way." The turtle immediately turned right, and Huang Zhe followed. After traveling more than ten miles, they arrived at a stream where they saw merchants sailing. Huang Zhe went to beg for food and spoke to the boatmen, saying, "I saw a turtle by the stream earlier. It was very large. We can go together to catch it." After he finished speaking, his face immediately broke out in sores. After going, they also did not see the turtle. After returning home for several days, he died from the sores.
